title: Ceropegia linearior
taxonomy: Ceropegia linearior is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as South Africa and Mozambique. It belongs to the genus Ceropegia, which consists of various species of leafy vines and twining plants. Ceropegia linearior is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, a group of plants known for their unique flower structures. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ceropegia linearior shares characteristics with other related plants, such as milkweeds and periwinkles.
